title: Pacific Alliance (PA)
abstract: This chapter provides an in-depth analysis of the Pacific Alliance, a regional integration initiative in Latin America that includes Colombia, Chile, Mexico, and Peru. The chapter begins by outlining the historical background and objectives of the PA, emphasising its goal of fostering economic integration, enhancing competitiveness, and promoting free trade within the region and with the Asia-Pacific. Through the use of principal component analysis (PCA), the chapter quantitatively assesses the level of integration among member countries using macroeconomic indicators from 1991 to 2019. The findings reveal that, while there is an upward trend in economic integration, the impacts are largely driven by global economic trends rather than the PA itself. The study highlights significant disparities in the economic profiles of the member countries, suggesting limited success in achieving deep integration. Nonetheless, the chapter underscores the potential of the PA, given its relatively recent establishment, and calls for continued efforts to enhance financial integration and cooperation among the member states.
